negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: Two explicit verbatim exclusions found at NYU Grossman hub: (a) non-LCME-approved medical schools cannot apply, (b) observer category not offered. The Bellevue rotation goes through NYU's domestic visiting-students lane only. There is no separate Bellevue-direct visiting-students lane on nychealthandhospitals.org.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Bellevue is not named on NYU's main visiting-MD-students hub page; only on the per-department elective pages (Advanced Medicine Sub-I, Ophthalmology). Visa absent from hub.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) SCHOOL_LEVEL_SOURCE — NYU Grossman SOM hub; Bellevue is reachable via NYU's domestic visiting-students lane only; (b) US LCME/COCA final-year only; (c) non-LCME EXCLUDED explicitly; (d) NO OBSERVER CATEGORY (no IMG-grad lane); (e) $225 registration fee; (f) max 1 four-week elective.
plainEnglishSummary: Bellevue Hospital is part of NYC Health + Hospitals but its visiting-medical-student access is controlled by NYU Grossman SOM. Only final-year US LCME or COCA medical students can apply via NYU's domestic visiting-students lane. International medical schools and observer categories are explicitly excluded — no IMG observership at Bellevue through this pathway. $225 registration fee, max 1 four-week elective. Bellevue is named as a rotation site for the Advanced Medicine Sub-I.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Bellevue was correctly classified as US-LCME-only via NYU rather than overclaimed as an IMG-friendly safety-net hospital; verbatim 'no observer category' protects against future IMG-friendly overclaim on Bellevue.
